Case Narrative

In brief, for the investigating officer:

  • A total of Rs 21,064,046.10 moved across the accounts in this case, of which Rs 8,284,635.43 entered the suspect network from outside.
  • The largest source of funds was account 48880432099033, which sent out Rs 2,775,120.76.
  • Money was routed through 1 intermediary account that received funds and quickly passed them on.
  • GAURAV KHANNA with account 68699629400471 spread money to 614 different accounts.
  • Account 48880432099033 collected money from 138 different accounts.
  • Rs 1,138,297.22 of incoming funds was traced to onward transfers or withdrawals.
  • The account to investigate first is 48880432099033, which shows the strongest combination of suspicious behaviors in this case.

4 · Findings by Account

acct_001 weak
acct_001 · Federal Bank · Account Number 48880432099033 · Score 218
Rank #1

The account acct_001 is suspicious due to an extreme credit of €294000.24, which was fully allocated across 5 debits with no remaining untraced amount. This unusual money trail, combined with other patterns, warrants further investigation. The credited amount and subsequent debits suggest potentially coordinated transactions.

  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit of €294000.24 was made to account acct_001, which was then fully allocated across 5 debits with no remaining untraced amount, indicating a potentially suspicious money trail.
  • Cross Statement Links strong — A matched flow link between debit and credit transactions was found based on an exact reference match with a confidence score of 1.0, and a shared reference '49652837781' provides additional corroboration for the link.
  • Shared Upi Identifiers strong — The shared UPI identifier 'msedclpayment@upi' is used by 2 accounts, including acct_001, which may indicate coordination or shared control between these accounts.
  • First Contact Large Transfer strong — A large transfer of €283360.15 was made from acct_001 to acct_007 on 2025-08-08, notable due to the lack of prior transactions between the accounts.
  • Money Flow Graph Construction structural — A money-flow graph has been constructed with 2141 nodes and 3697 directed transaction edges, indicating a complex network of financial transactions across multiple accounts, including acct_001, acct_002, acct_003, acct_004, and acct_007, with all transactions denominated in ₹ INR. The investigative significance of this finding lies in the potential for identifying patterns of money laundering or other illicit financial activities, given the high volume and complexity of transactions involved.

acct_002 · HDFC BANK LTD · Account Number 68699629400471 · Score 125
Rank #3

This account is suspicious due to a large transfer of ₹284358.16 from acct_007 to acct_002 on 2025-08-09 with no prior relationship. The money trail tracing pattern identified a credit transaction of ₹284358.16 allocated to two subsequent debit transactions. The account's high-throughput pass-through behavior and complex money flow graph also raise concerns.

  • First Contact Large Transfer strong — A large transfer of ₹284358.16 occurred from acct_007 to acct_002 on 2025-08-09 with no prior relationship. (+1 more of this type)
  • Money Trail Tracing strong — A credit transaction of ₹284358.16 was allocated to two subsequent debit transactions, leaving no remaining untraced amount.
  • Cross Statement Links weak — A direct financial transaction occurred between acct_007 and acct_002, with a debit from acct_007 matching a credit to acct_002. (+1 more of this type)
  • Pass Through Routing Account weak — Account acct_002 received ₹4419609.60 from 133 sources and disbursed ₹4258306.89 to 614 targets, retaining only 3.6% of inflow — consistent with high-throughput pass-through. The large number of transactions and low retention rate suggest that this account may be used for pass-through transactions, potentially indicating a money laundering scheme.

Data Security & Method (Appendix)

What stays on this machine

  • All fraud detection, scoring and ranking run on this machine (deterministic code).
  • Raw bank statements and extracted transactions never leave the machine.
  • The money-flow graph, clusters and suspicion scores are computed locally.

What is sent to an external service

  • Only small, redacted text samples are sent to the language model to describe a statement's column layout when local parsing is insufficient.
  • For images / scanned PDFs, the page pixels are sent to a vision model (the one unavoidable case).
  • The language model only rewrites explanations into plain language; it never changes a number, account, or decision.
